summary: Oracle Health and Life Sciences supports the HTI-5 proposed deregulatory actions, emphasizing the need to reduce certification burdens to foster innovation and interoperability. They highlight the benefits of removing outdated requirements like C-CDA creation and easing audit log and EHI export mandates.
key_arguments: Reducing certification requirements will lower development costs and speed innovation.
Removing outdated standards like C-CDA creation aligns with modern interoperability needs.
Easing audit log and EHI export requirements reduces unnecessary burden without compromising security.
Simplifying USCDI data class requirements supports more flexible data exchange.
recommendations: Proceed with removing the C-CDA creation certification criterion.
Reduce audit log and EHI export requirements as proposed.
Maintain focus on burden reduction to encourage health IT innovation.
Ensure any deregulatory actions do not compromise patient safety or data security.
notable_quote: Reducing certification requirements will lower costs and speed innovation while maintaining security.
